title: Learn a Language
description: AI writes code for you — but understanding what it wrote is what makes you dangerous. Pick a language, learn the fundamentals, and go from copying prompts to actually knowing what's happening.
outcomes: Read and understand code that AI generates for you
Modify AI-generated code confidently — add features, fix bugs, change behavior
Write simple programs from scratch when AI isn't available
Know which language to pick for different types of projects

recommendedPaths.title: Learn Python
recommendedPaths.reason: The most beginner-friendly language. Perfect for automation, data work, and AI. If you're not sure where to start, start here.

recommendedPaths.title: Learn JavaScript
recommendedPaths.reason: The language of the web. If you want to build websites, web apps, or anything that runs in a browser, this is your language.

recommendedPaths.title: Learn HTML & CSS
recommendedPaths.reason: Not programming languages, but the foundation of every website. Learn these first if you want to build anything visual on the web.

concepts.name: Variables and Types
concepts.explanation: Variables are named containers that hold data — a number, some text, a list. Types tell the language what kind of data it is, which determines what you can do with it.

concepts.name: Functions
concepts.explanation: Functions are reusable blocks of code. Instead of writing the same logic over and over, you write it once as a function and call it whenever you need it.

concepts.name: Control Flow
concepts.explanation: If/else statements and loops let your code make decisions and repeat actions. This is how programs handle different situations — 'if the user is logged in, show the dashboard; otherwise, show the login page.'

tools.name: Cursor
tools.description: AI-first code editor — learn by building with AI assistance

tools.name: Claude Code
tools.description: Terminal-based AI coding — great for learning how programs work

tools.name: VS Code
tools.description: The standard free code editor — works with every language
